growth_records.strain: S. multivorans DSM 12446 / D. mccartyi BTF08
growth_records.medium: Bicarbonate-buffered mineral salt medium, lactate donor, + vitamin B12

up.method: GC; net dechlorination rate PCE->ethene of Sm/BTF08 co-culture (Table 1); early PCE->cDCE ~30 umol/bottle/day
HPLC; consumed continuously, completely consumed by day 12 then re-fed (Fig 1E); numeric rate not stated

sec.method: HPLC; acetate production occurred continuously during the whole dechlorination process (Fig 1E); from S. multivorans lactate fermentation; numeric rate not stated

growth_records.cite: Stefan Kruse et al. Interspecies metabolite transfer and aggregate formation in a co-culture of Dehalococcoides and Sulfurospirillum dehalogenating tetrachloroethene to ethene. The ISME Journal (2021). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-00887-6. [PMC8163811]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1: Sm/BTF08, electron donor Lactate, With vitamin B12, Dechlorination rate 41 +/- 2 uM day-1. 'D. mccartyi strain BTF08 needed 7 days for one cell doubling in the co-culture.' S. multivorans: 'at least one cell doubling per day'.
growth_records.notes: Volumetric dechlorination rate (uM/day), not per-gDW. Growth: doubling_time_h=168 h taken from 'BTF08 needed 7 days for one cell doubling' -> mu=ln2/168=0.693/168=0.004126 /h (calculated) for the D. mccartyi partner. S. multivorans partner grew faster: 'at least one cell doubling per day' (td<=24 h -> mu>=ln2/24=0.0289 /h) during PCE->cDCE respiratory phase, then 'less than one cell doubling in 20 days' during lactate-fermentation phase. Dechlorination in co-culture was 4.5-fold faster than BTF08 pure culture. Lactate consumed and acetate produced (Fig 1E) but no numeric rates given. PCE consumed=ethene produced stoichiometrically at 41 uM/day.

growth_records.medium: Bicarbonate-buffered mineral salt medium, lactate donor, + vitamin B12

up.method: GC; net dechlorination rate PCE->VC of Sm/195 co-culture (Table 1)

sec.compound: VC (vinyl chloride, terminal product for strain 195)

sec.method: HPLC; produced continuously from S. multivorans lactate fermentation (Fig 1F); numeric rate not stated

growth_records.cite: Stefan Kruse et al. Interspecies metabolite transfer and aggregate formation in a co-culture of Dehalococcoides and Sulfurospirillum dehalogenating tetrachloroethene to ethene. The ISME Journal (2021). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-00887-6. [PMC8163811]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1: Sm/195, electron donor Lactate, With vitamin B12, Dechlorination rate 48 +/- 1 uM day-1. 'The Sm/195 co-culture reduced PCE to VC more than threefold faster than the D. mccartyi strain 195 pure culture, which completed dechlorinati
growth_records.notes: Volumetric dechlorination rate (uM/day). Strain 195 produces VC as terminal product. PCE consumed=VC produced stoichiometrically at 48 uM/day. Co-culture ~3-fold faster than 195 pure culture. Lactate consumed / acetate produced (Fig 1F) not numerically quantified. No explicit growth rate reported for this specific set-up.

growth_records.medium: Bicarbonate-buffered mineral salt medium, lactate donor, WITHOUT vitamin B12

up.method: GC; dechlorination rate PCE->VC (Table 1, without vitamin B12)

growth_records.cite: Stefan Kruse et al. Interspecies metabolite transfer and aggregate formation in a co-culture of Dehalococcoides and Sulfurospirillum dehalogenating tetrachloroethene to ethene. The ISME Journal (2021). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-00887-6. [PMC8163811]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1: Sm/195, electron donor Lactate, Without vitamin B12, Dechlorination rate 9 +/- 0.04 uM day-1. 'the cDCE to VC dechlorination rate increased fourfold (3.6 umol/bottle/day)' after PCE re-feeding.
growth_records.notes: Volumetric dechlorination rate (uM/day) without exogenous vitamin B12; D. mccartyi 195 dechlorinates using norpseudo-B12 produced by S. multivorans. Much slower (9 vs 48 uM/day) than +B12 set-up. PCE consumed=VC produced. No growth rate reported.

growth_records.strain: S. multivorans DSM 12446 / D. mccartyi BTF08
growth_records.medium: Bicarbonate-buffered mineral salt medium, lactate donor, WITHOUT vitamin B12 / with DMB (5,6-dimethylbenzimidazole)

up.method: GC; dechlorination rate PCE->ethene (Table 1, without B12 / with DMB); cDCE->ethene 36 +/- 2 uM/day

sec.method: GC; ethene production rate from cDCE (Table 1)

growth_records.cite: Stefan Kruse et al. Interspecies metabolite transfer and aggregate formation in a co-culture of Dehalococcoides and Sulfurospirillum dehalogenating tetrachloroethene to ethene. The ISME Journal (2021). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-00887-6. [PMC8163811]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1: Sm/BTF08, Lactate, Without vitamin B12/with DMB, 36 +/- 2 uM day-1. 'Ethene production rates from cDCE (36 +/- 2 uM/day) were similar to the co-culture amended with vitamin B12 (41 +/- 2 uM/day). The dechlorination rate increased a
growth_records.notes: Volumetric dechlorination rate (uM/day) with DMB lower-ligand instead of complete vitamin B12; BTF08 can build a functional cobamide from DMB, giving near-full activity (36 vs 41 uM/day). PCE consumed=ethene produced. No growth rate reported.

growth_records.medium: Bicarbonate-buffered mineral salt medium, lactate donor, WITHOUT vitamin B12 / with DMB

up.method: GC; dechlorination rate PCE->VC (Table 1, without B12 / with DMB)

growth_records.cite: Stefan Kruse et al. Interspecies metabolite transfer and aggregate formation in a co-culture of Dehalococcoides and Sulfurospirillum dehalogenating tetrachloroethene to ethene. The ISME Journal (2021). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-00887-6. [PMC8163811]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1: Sm/195, Lactate, Without vitamin B12/with DMB, Dechlorination rate 8 +/- 0.5 uM day-1.
growth_records.notes: Volumetric dechlorination rate (uM/day) with DMB lower ligand instead of complete vitamin B12 for the Sm/195 co-culture; low rate (8 uM/day), consistent with 195 requiring specific cobamide types. PCE consumed=VC produced. No growth rate reported. Note: the Sm/BTF08 co-culture WITHOUT vitamin B12 (no DMB) showed no dechlorination ('-' in Table 1) and is therefore not recorded.
